engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor decides by browsing photos, menu, wine selection, room descriptions, hours and directions of a physical hotel-restaurant — the place itself is what's being evaluated.

goal.one_action: Reserve a table / room (or a weekend/romantic package) at the hotel-restaurant

structure.archetype_note: Classic multi-page hospitality brochure: separate pages per facet (restaurant, hotel, menu, wine, events) plus contact; primary CTA 'Reserva ara' redirects to Avaibook, phone numbers and a contact form as secondary paths.

mapped.observed_as: Eating, resting and experiencing are the three verbs
Cap de Setmana al Bosc / Romantic Night Package (gift boxes)
Owner & Wine Program — Jordi Raventós, sommelier, 2008 Golden Nose

unmapped.why_no_fit: No canonical section id for a restaurant dish/menu listing; menu is a place-specific structural block
map-hours is a capability but there is no canonical section id for a location/hours block

proposed_new.name: external-booking-redirect
proposed_new.evidence: Primary 'Reserva ara' CTA hands off to the Avaibook third-party booking engine; no on-site scheduling inventory — rooms/tables/rates all handled externally

confidence.why: fetch_quality full; clear multi-page hospitality structure, CTAs, forms and embeds all captured

gap_findings: No canonical section id for a restaurant menu/dish list (prices absent here) — recurring place-type gap
No canonical section for a location/opening-hours block despite map-hours being a capability

Place engine juggles three co-equal conversion paths (external booking redirect + tel + contact form); taxonomy's single one_action forces a primary pick that understates the multi-CTA reality of hospitality sites
